purpose: Issue short-lived upload tokens, store only their hashes, and validate presented tokens for direct or proxied file upload workflows.

endpoints.description: Create a one-time or time-limited upload token and return the plaintext token once.
Verify a presented upload token against its stored hash and expiration window.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Short-lived upload token issuance and verification using hashed token storage.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a caller needs a one-time or short-lived upload authorization token rather than permanent credentials.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a general login session token, OAuth access token, or permanent API key.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Create an upload token bound to a target path. 2) Return the plaintext token once to the client. 3) Verify the token before accepting uploaded bytes.

failure_modes.condition: Token is expired or already consumed
failure_modes.recovery: Issue a fresh token and retry the upload

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls
Must not persist plaintext upload tokens

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Generate upload tokens with crypto.randomBytes or randomUUID-derived randomness, hash them with SHA-256 before persistence, and compare with timingSafeEqual where possible. Bind each token to a target path and expiry.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Returning tokens more than once or storing plaintext tokens expands the blast radius of any database leak. Expired tokens should be cleaned up opportunistically.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Store token_id, token_hash, target_path, expires_at, and consumed_at. Return plaintext token only on creation.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not derive tokens from predictable IDs, secrets, or filenames.
